artworks.title: Between Worlds
artworks.description: A luminous figure stands at the threshold between the life she has known and the self she is becoming. Watched over by otherworldly guides, she does not ask permission to cross. This is the sacred pause before transformation --- when fear loosens its hold and the unknown begins to feel like home.

artworks.title: The Woman You Keep Hidden
artworks.description: Inside every carefully constructed life, an untamed woman waits. She is bold, inconvenient and wholly alive - the self we silence to remain acceptable. Set upon a chessboard, the work confronts our desire to calculate every move while the soul refuses to be controlled. Let her emerge. Let yourself be seen. Wholeness begins where hiding ends.

artworks.title: The Art of Not Falling Apart
artworks.description: A meditation on the invisible architecture of survival. What appears fragile is held together by instinct, memory and a thousand private acts of courage. The work does not romanticise breaking; it honours the strange, beautiful intelligence with which we gather our pieces and continue.

artworks.title: Sacred Guardians
artworks.description: Protective presences emerge from colour like memories older than language. Neither fully human nor entirely mythical, they stand between the vulnerable self and the darkness beyond it. A talismanic work about intuition, ancestry and the unseen forces that carry us when we cannot carry ourselves.

artworks.title: The Gold Came After
artworks.description: The fracture came first. The gold arrived later --- not to erase what happened, but to reveal where healing had taken place. Inspired by the beauty of repaired things, this intimate work is a testament to becoming more precious through survival: wounded, transformed and impossible to diminish.

artworks.title: Transition
artworks.description: A body of colour moves between an ending and a beginning. Old identities dissolve; new forms have not yet found their names. Transition inhabits that uncertain, fertile space where control disappears and transformation quietly takes command. It is not a painting about arrival, but the courage to remain in motion.

artworks.title: Itachi
artworks.description: A portrait of devotion concealed beneath distance. Itachi explores the unbearable tension between love and sacrifice, tenderness and duty, the face we show and the truth we carry alone. The work honours those whose deepest acts of love are misunderstood --- and the silence such love demands.

artworks.title: The Loudest Soul in the Room
artworks.description: Some presences need no permission and no volume. They alter the atmosphere simply by existing without apology. This work celebrates the magnetic self --- instinctive, vivid and impossible to reduce --- whose truth enters a room before language does.

artworks.title: Love Is the Beginning. Love Is the End.
artworks.description: Before fear, there is love. After every loss, love remains. This work holds love not as sentiment, but as the primal force beneath creation, grief and return --- the first language we recognise and the final truth we carry with us.

artworks.title: Between Fire and Water
artworks.description: Two elemental forces hold one another in exquisite tension: fire, with its hunger to become; water, with its wisdom to surrender. Neither conquers the other. Together they create balance --- a diptych about passion and stillness, resistance and release, and the self that emerges when opposites are finally allowed to coexist.

artworks.title: Heidendom
artworks.description: Ancient instinct rises beneath the polished surface of the present. Heidendom reaches toward ritual, earth and the sacred wild --- a world in which the body remembers what civilisation asked it to forget. Small in scale yet charged with primal energy, it is an invitation back to origin.

artworks.title: Hope
artworks.description: Hope is not delicate optimism; it is the stubborn upward movement of life after devastation. Carved into wood, the figure carries the memory of resistance in every mark. Rising without denying the weight below, the sculpture gives endurance a body --- grounded, scarred and still reaching toward light.
